title: Fitness App
tagline: A training product narrowed specifically for gym users.
description: This is the product I am currently building. The goal is not to make a bigger fitness app, but to reduce the experience down to the core tasks that truly matter inside a gym workflow.
goal: Build a lighter, more direct, lower-noise product loop around the single scenario of gym training.
role: User research, product definition, requirements writing, AI-assisted development
targetUsers: Consistency-focused lifters who want fewer choices
People who care more about training efficiency than feature overload
problem: Many fitness apps serve an overly broad audience. Features keep expanding, while the core tasks of serious gym users become diluted.
solution: I am starting from subtraction: collect real demand, use AI to connect product definition with documents and code delivery, and narrow the experience around the most frequent jobs of gym users.
features: A tighter feature set centered on gym training instead of broad lifestyle fitness
Continuous user-demand capture and synthesis through AI crawling
A markdown-first delivery flow that shortens the distance between design intent and code
Priority given to high-frequency jobs such as workout logging, plan review, and key feedback
challenges: Keeping strong training value while removing a large amount of non-essential scope
Turning scattered complaints and requests into high-confidence product judgement
Making AI-generated documents, design instructions, and code share the same product meaning
learnings: The more vertical the user scenario, the more useful it is to start with one tightly defined task loop.
The biggest early value of AI is not only speed, but continuity across demand, documentation, and implementation.

title: AIPM Blog
tagline: A content system for projects, reading, methods, and personal brand storytelling.
description: This blog is more than an about page. It is the structured home for my projects, books, AI product workflow, and the longer arc of how I am growing as a builder.
goal: Unify personal brand expression, content management, and project storytelling inside one maintainable content model.
role: Content modeling, frontend implementation, brand storytelling
targetUsers: People who want to understand what I am building
problem: Many personal sites only show fragmented information. Projects, reading, methods, and direction end up disconnected, and the site becomes hard to maintain over time.
solution: I split personal profile, projects, books, and capabilities into structured content layers, then built one page system around them so the site can express the present and keep scaling with future content.
features: Independent schemas for profile, projects, books, and skills to keep updates manageable
Locally managed SVG book covers for a more unified visual system and easier asset maintenance
A dedicated module for the AI-native product workflow so it can keep evolving over time
Parallel Chinese and English content support for future-facing presentation
challenges: Keeping personal expression warm while still making the content system structured and reusable
Avoiding a one-off personal-brand refresh and turning the site into a true long-term workspace
learnings: The most convincing part of a personal brand is not a slogan, but consistently updated projects and methods.
Once content is modeled first, redesigning, expanding, and repackaging later becomes much easier.
